Tonight, as the storm is coming, I feel the weight of the world pressing down on my chest, and I'm not sure I'll survive it. But I'm writing this to you because I need to say that I'm sorry, and I need to know that you're still in my thoughts.

**To My Dearest Eleanor,**

I write these words with trembling hands and a heart heavy with years of silence. The sea has been my only companion these past five years, and now, as the tempest looms, I find myself unmoored, both by the storm and by the absence of your voice.

You were once my light, my guiding star in the vast, dark night of this world. Now, with the wind howling like a mournful dirge, I find myself yearning for the warmth of your embrace, the laughter that once filled our home.

I have not spoken these words in five years, yet the silence has been a prison, and I have not been able to find the words to call you back. But tonight, as the storm gathers strength, I find myself standing at the edge of the world, with the lighthouse as my shield, and the sea as my witness.

I do not know if I shall survive the night, but I do know that I would give my life to keep the light burning, to guide the way for any soul that may find shelter in these walls.

You were once my daughter, and now, I am but a mere shadow of what we once were. Yet, I have not forgotten the love that once bound us, and the promise that I made to you, that you would return, as the storm would pass.

**Letter from the Lighthouse Keeper to His Estranged Daughter**

My Dearest Eleanor,

It has been five long years since I last laid eyes upon thine, and yet I have not spoken thy name in all that time. The sea has whispered of thee, and the wind has carried tales of thee, but I have not dared to write a word to thee, for fear that the ink may not hold the weight of my heart.

The stars have been my only companion, and the waves have been my only song. I have lived in solitude, and yet the silence has been a cruel companion. But tonight, the storm is upon us, and the sea shall rise with a fury I have not known in all my years.

The tempest is upon the shore, and the lighthouse is in peril. I have not the strength to brave the storm, and I fear that the sea may take me, and that I may not see thee again.

Yet, I have not forgotten thee, and I have not ceased to think of thee. The sea may take me, but I have not ceased to yearn for thee. I have not the strength to fight the storm, and yet I shall not let the sea take me without a word to thee.

I have written this with my last breath, and I have not the strength to write more. For I have not the strength to live, and yet I shall not let the storm take me without a word to thee.

My Dearest
